## Overview

`MultiOpt` is an R package for running simulated annealing to select
individuals best suited to the users priorities..

The package was developed to support analyses presented in:

> Author A, Author B, and Author C. (Year). *Title of manuscript*.
> Journal Name. DOI: xxx

Website: [Here](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/index.html) \##
Installation

### Development version

``` r

install.packages("remotes")

remotes::install_github("herz6627/MultiOpt")
```

## Getting Started

Load the package:

``` r

library(MultiOpt)
```

Basic example:

Generate some trait data to use

``` r

set.seed(12345)
n = 100
x = rnorm(n = n, mean = 120, sd = 2)
y = x * 3 + rnorm(n = n, mean = 0, sd = 20)
dat = data.frame(x = x, y = y)
```

Format trait data to a list of matrices

``` r

trait_list = list(
x = as.matrix(dat$x),
y = as.matrix(dat$y)
)
```

Here is where we specify how we want to measure success. This needs to
be a list of functions as listed in ?multiopt_sa(). Full details are can
be found in the XXXX vignette.

``` r

measure_list = list(
 x = weighted_mean_of_vector,
 y = weighted_mean_of_vector
)
```

And we can specify and additional arguments to the selected functions in
`measure_list`. Some of the functions require these additional
arguments, which can be found in the respective function descriptions,
but a key thing to note is that this is the location to specify which
direction you want to optimize (maximize or minimize). By default,
MultiOpt maximizes a trait (direction = 1). Use direction = -1 to get
the minimum instead as we do here for trait `y`.

``` r

args_list = list(
 x = NULL,
 y = list(direction = -1)
)
```

Note: across these three lists, all trait names need to match for list
elements.

It is important for simulated annealing that all traits being optimized
are on the same scale. If trait values are not scaled, larger values
will be prioritized by the algorithm. trait_list_scaled() will scale all
provided traits to 0-1.

``` r

trait_list_scaled <- scale_traits(trait_list)
```

Simulated annealing is a stochastic algorithm. As such, it is good to
run the algorithm a few times to get an idea of how stable the results
are, which we can do with
[`rand_multiopt()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/rand_multiopt.md)
which requires a list of inputs to specify our simulated annealing
parameters.
[`multiopt_sa()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/multiopt_sa.md)
will run a single round of simulated annealing and has slightly
different formatting.

``` r

sa_args = list(
trait_list = trait_list_scaled, # our list of traits
measure_list = measure_list, # our list of measures
measure_args_list = args_list, # our list of arguments for each measure
n_t = 10, # how many individuals we want in our final population
weights_max = 1, # the maximum number of times an individual can be selected
max_steps = 5000 # how many time steps to run each simulated annealing
)
```

And now we can run simulated annealing

``` r

test = rand_multiopt(
n_runs = 5, # how many times to replicate the algorithm
multiopt_args = sa_args# all of our arguments
)
str(test)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Package index

## All functions

- [`accept_reject()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/accept_reject.md)
  : Determines acceptance or rejection of a proposal
- [`allele_enrichment()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/allele_enrichment.md)
  : Desirable allele enrichment
- [`calculate_measure()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/calculate_measure.md)
  : Apply measure functions across multiple trait datasets
- [`check_similar_scale()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/check_similar_scale.md)
  : Check trait data scaling
- [`clean_archive()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/clean_archive.md)
  : Reconcile a candidate solution with a non-dominated (Pareto) archive
- [`combine_archives()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/combine_archives.md)
  : Combine two archives and reduce if needed
- [`.check_weights()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/dot-check_weights.md)
  : Internal helper for validating weight constraints.
- [`explore_pareto()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/explore_pareto.md)
  : Explore Pareto Front
- [`min_max_scale()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/min_max_scale.md)
  : Min-max scale
- [`min_max_unscale()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/min_max_unscale.md)
  : Reverse min-max scaling using a reference dataset
- [`modify_weights()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/modify_weights.md)
  : Modify weight vector
- [`multiopt_sa()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/multiopt_sa.md)
  : Multi-objective simulated annealing optimization
- [`nei_diversity()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/nei_diversity.md)
  : Nei diversity
- [`plot_chain()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/plot_chain.md)
  : Plot trait value chains over iterations
- [`plot_pareto()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/plot_pareto.md)
  : Visualize pairwise Pareto trade-offs
- [`plot_selection()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/plot_selection.md)
  : Visualize selection frequency across individuals and traits
- [`plot_selection_single()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/plot_selection_single.md)
  : Visualize trait values of selected individuals
- [`propose_initial_weights()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/propose_initial_weights.md)
  : Generate initial weights
- [`rand_multiopt()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/rand_multiopt.md)
  : Multiple runs of simulated annealing
- [`scale_traits()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/scale_traits.md)
  : Min-max scale trait datasets to the range \[0, 1\]
- [`shannon_diversity()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/shannon_diversity.md)
  : Shannon diversity
- [`singleopt_context()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/singleopt_context.md)
  : Run single-objective simulated annealing in context of additional
  traits
- [`sum_of_squared_difference()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/sum_of_squared_difference.md)
  : \#' Compute weighted sum of squared deviations from a target value
- [`temp_scheduler()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/temp_scheduler.md)
  : Determine annealing temperature
- [`unscale_archive()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/unscale_archive.md)
  : Back transform output to original scale
- [`unscale_multiopt()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/unscale_multiopt.md)
  : Back transform output to original scale
- [`unscale_rand_multiopt()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/unscale_rand_multiopt.md)
  : Back transform output to original scale
- [`unscale_singleopt()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/unscale_singleopt.md)
  : Back transform output to original scale
- [`weighted_mean_of_absolute_difference()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/weighted_mean_of_absolute_difference.md)
  : Compute weighted mean absolute deviation from a target value
- [`weighted_mean_of_pairwise_matrix()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/weighted_mean_of_pairwise_matrix.md)
  : Compute weighted mean pairwise similarity (or distance)
- [`weighted_mean_of_vector()`](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/reference/weighted_mean_of_vector.md)
  : Compute weighted deviation of a vector from a target value

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [overview](https://herz6627.github.io/MultiOpt/articles/overview.md):
